what_was_done: Verified wrapup indexer fix from session 214 and found SECOND performance bug. When wrapup status file had no commit_hash (from stuck/failed run), the HEAD~1 incremental fallback only applied to boot, not wrapup — causing wrapup to full-reindex all ~3000 chunks every time (5+ min). Fixed by changing `elif snapshot_type == 'boot':` to `else:` in memory_server.py line 1404. Also confirmed boot indexer showing 0 chunks is correct incremental behavior.
next_steps: Verify wrapup indexer runs fast after fix (restart MCP server to pick up new code)
Build Claude API adapter for agent-bench (real API scoring)
known_issues: Plan mode exit loop - platform limitation, mitigated by behavioral rule
ChromaDB concurrent access - tests skip when MCP running, correct behavior
test_framework.py Pyright import errors for shared modules (path resolution)
